channel.title: The Olsen Tribune
channel.description: Daily business intelligence — markets, technology, healthcare, politics, and analysis.

item.title: Equity Complacency Collides With a Harder Reality in the Gulf
item.description: Investors celebrating an open Strait of Hormuz may be pricing only half the story as damaged infrastructure and fractured logistics reshape the regional risk premium.

item.title: Seattle's Data Center Pushback Signals a New Cost of Capital for the AI Build-Out
item.description: Mayor Katie Wilson's openness to a moratorium on large server facilities underscores a municipal backlash that is quietly reshaping the economics of hyperscale infrastructure.

item.title: Samsung Bets on Software Cadence to Defend Premium Smartphone Turf
item.description: By pushing flagship-tier features down to older Galaxy handsets through a sweeping One UI refresh, Samsung is reframing what loyalty means in a saturated phone market.

item.title: Microsoft Tests the Limits of All-You-Can-Play in Xbox Game Pass Overhaul
item.description: A leaked tier featuring metered cloud gaming hours suggests Redmond is quietly retreating from the buffet model that defined a decade of subscription gaming.

item.title: Experimental Therapy Frees Liver Recipients From Lifelong Drug Regimens
item.description: A small but pivotal trial suggests transplant medicine may be on the verge of solving its most persistent problem: the patient's own immune system.

item.title: When the Chatbot Gets It Right: AI's Quiet Challenge to Diagnostic Medicine
item.description: A growing number of patients are turning to consumer AI tools after their doctors come up empty, raising uncomfortable questions for an industry unprepared for the shift.

item.title: Tehran's Hormuz Gambit Forces Washington Into a Corner
item.description: Iran's decision to seal the world's most critical oil chokepoint and fire on commercial vessels has transformed a diplomatic standoff into a test of American resolve.

item.title: Trump's Psychedelics Order Upends Decades of Federal Drug Policy
item.description: An executive directive fast-tracking federal review of psilocybin and ibogaine signals a remarkable bipartisan realignment around mental health treatment.

item.title: Seattle's Data Center Reckoning Exposes the Hidden Costs of the AI Boom
item.description: As Mayor Katie Wilson weighs a moratorium on new hyperscale facilities, the city becomes the latest flashpoint in a national debate over who pays for the power behind artificial intelligence.

item.title: Markets May Be Misreading the Real Economic Fallout of the Iran Conflict
item.description: While traders fixate on the Strait of Hormuz, quieter disruptions to supply chains and regional infrastructure could prove more consequential than the headline oil price suggests.

item.title: Treasury Yields Retreat as March Jobs Report Underwhelms Expectations
item.description: Nonfarm payrolls added 142,000 positions last month, falling short of the 185,000 consensus, intensifying debate over the labor market's trajectory.

item.title: Stripe Expands Into Embedded Banking, Challenging Traditional Financial Infrastructure
item.description: The payments giant unveiled a suite of banking-as-a-service tools that allow platforms to offer deposit accounts and lending products directly to their merchants.

item.title: Why Private Credit's Rapid Growth Is Drawing Comparisons to Pre-2008 Shadow Banking
item.description: The $1.7 trillion private credit market has doubled in three years. Regulators and institutional investors are beginning to ask uncomfortable questions about underwriting standards and liquidity risk.

item.title: Copper Prices Hit Record Highs on Supply Constraints and Electrification Demand
item.description: LME copper surpassed $10,200 per metric ton as mining output lags behind consumption driven by electric vehicles, renewable energy infrastructure, and data center expansion.

item.title: Satya Nadella on Building an AI-Native Organization: "Culture Eats Strategy"
item.description: In an interview with The Olsen Tribune, the Microsoft CEO discussed the organizational shifts required to embed artificial intelligence into every product team.

item.title: Federal Reserve Signals Patience on Rate Cuts as Inflation Data Remains Uneven
item.description: Central bank officials acknowledged progress on price stability but emphasized the need for "sustained evidence" before adjusting the federal funds rate.

item.title: Anthropic's Latest Funding Round Values AI Startup at $60 Billion
item.description: The safety-focused artificial intelligence company closed a $3.5 billion round led by existing investors, underscoring sustained institutional appetite for foundation model developers.

item.title: Inside Jensen Huang's Playbook for Navigating the GPU Supply Crunch
item.description: Nvidia's CEO is rethinking allocation strategy as hyperscaler demand outpaces production capacity, creating ripple effects across the AI infrastructure stack.

item.title: The Coming Reckoning in Commercial Real Estate: What the Data Shows
item.description: Office vacancy rates in major metros have climbed past 20 percent, and a wall of maturities looms in 2026. We examine which markets face the steepest repricing.

item.title: Databricks Crosses $2.4B ARR, Fueling Speculation of a 2026 IPO
item.description: The data analytics platform's revenue growth has accelerated for three consecutive quarters, putting it squarely in the conversation for the year's most anticipated public offering.
